summary: Fowler addresses the common problem of presentation slides having text and diagrams too small for audience members to read, especially those sitting further back. He offers a simple, practical technique: compose presentations at 50% view size. If you can't easily read your own slides at half size, your audience will struggle during the actual presentation. This serves as a quick self-check before presenting.
key_points: Small text and diagrams are a widespread problem in presentation decks
People in the back of the room suffer most from undersized content
Composing at 50% view size simulates audience viewing conditions
If you struggle to read it at half size, your audience will too
money_quotes: A common problem in people's presentation decks is that they make text and diagrams so small that only the people in the front of the room can properly see them.
If I can't read it easily at 50%, then the audience will struggle too.
key_insight: Test your presentation readability by composing at 50% view size—if you can't read it easily, neither will your audience.
